zh.Sandford, Devon, England: 英國英格蘭戴文桑德福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Francis Lowe於1520年出生在英國戴文法林格頓時，他的父親Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF25歲，母親Anne Reynes24歲。他於1552年與Thomasine Farrington在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1594年在英國史洛普爾享年74歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾。

zh-hans.Sandford, Devon, England: 英国英格兰戴文桑德福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Francis Lowe于1520年出生在英国戴文法林格頓时，他的父亲Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF25岁，母亲Anne Reynes24岁。他于1552年与Thomasine Farrington在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1594年在英国史洛普爾享年74岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Lowe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Low  .

German (Löwe) and Danish (Løwe): from Middle High German lēwe, löuwe ‘lion’, hence a nickname for a brave or regal person. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. It is also found in Sweden. Compare Loewe 1, Lau , Lave , and Lurvey .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’.

variants.name: Lau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Wing, Kam, Kwok, Ming, Chi, Yuk, Chun, Kin, Ching, Kwan, Man.

German: nickname for a physically strong person, from Middle High German louwe, lauwe ‘lion’. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. Compare Lowe .
German: topographic name for someone living in a bush-covered area or clearing, Middle Low German lo, loch, lage, Middle High German lō.

variants.name: Loew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eckhard, Fritz, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Manfred, Raimund.
German (mainly Löw): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). The surname Loew is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Low 5.

variants.name: Lobe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Erwin, Horst, Otto, Winfried.
German (Löbe): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
German: metonymic occupational name for a tanner, from Middle High German lō ‘tanning bark’.

variants.name: Loewe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Armin, Gerhard, Helmuth, Klaus, Kurt.
German (mainly Löwe): see Lowe 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’. Compare Lowe 3.

variants.name: Loewen
meanings: German (Löwen): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). Compare Lowen 2.

variants.name: Low
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived near a tumulus, mound or hill, Middle English lowe, from Old English hlāw (see Law 2).

Scottish and English: nickname for a short man, from Middle English lah, lowe (Old Norse lágr; the word was adopted first into the northern dialects of Middle English, where Scandinavian influence was strong, and then spread south, with regular alteration of the vowel quality).

English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a violent or dangerous person, from Anglo-Norman French lou, leu ‘wolf’ (from Latin lupus). Wolves were relatively common in Britain at the time when most surnames were formed, as there still existed large tracts of uncleared forest.

variants.name: Lurvey
meanings: Americanized form of German Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
History: Peter Lurvy, the immigrant ancestor of this family, was in Ipswich, MA, by 1679.

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Francis Lowe was born in 1520, in Farringdon, Devon, England, his father, Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF, was 25 and his mother, Anne Reynes, was 24. He married Thomasine Farrington in 1552, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1594, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 74, and was buried in Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Francis Lowe est né en 1520, à Farringdon, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Anne Reynes, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Thomasine Farrington en 1552, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1594, à Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Francis Lowe 1520 in Farringdon, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF 25 und seine Mutter Anne Reynes 24. Er hat 1552 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Thomasine Farrington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1594 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Shropshire, England und wurde in Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Francis Lowe è nato il nel 1520 nel Farringdon, Devon, England, il suo padre, Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Anne Reynes, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Thomasine Farrington nel 1552, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1594, nel Shropshire, England, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Francis Loweが1520年にイギリスデボンファリングドンで生まれた時，父Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGFは25歳，母Anne Reynesは24歳であった。1552年にイギリスイギリスデボンでThomasine Farringtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1594年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで74歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Francis Lowe(이)가 1520년 영국 데폰 패링던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Reynes(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1552년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Thomasine Farrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1594년 영국 슈럽실르에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Francis Lowe nasceu em 1520, em Farringdon, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Anne Reynes, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Thomasine Farrington em 1552, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1594, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Francis Lowe родился в 1520 г. в местности фаррингдон, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Reynes, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Thomasine Farrington в 1552 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1594 г. в местности шропшир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Francis Lowe nació en 1520, en Farringdon, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Anne Reynes, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Thomasine Farrington en 1552, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1594, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Shropshire, Inglaterra.
